ab: tatiyaw na vaik aken a paljilji tjai vuvu a uqaljay.
ch: 昨天，我去部落拜訪爺爺。
en: Yesterday, I went to visit my grandpa in the tribe.

ab: cuacuai anga men a inika mecevung singlit aravac aken tjaimadju.
ch: 我們很久沒有見面了，我很想念他。
en: We haven’t seen each other for a long time and I miss him a lot.

ab: kapacun ti vuvu a uqaljay tjanuaken pakanan aken ta kudamunu sa patekelan aken ta utja.
ch: 爺爺一看到我，就請我吃西瓜，請我喝茶。
en: As soon as grandpa saw me, he invited me to eat watermelon and drink tea.

ab: qaljemeqem a sinitalem a kudamunu ni vuvu a uqaljay sanguaq angata.
ch: 爺爺種的西瓜很甜，真的很好吃。
en: The watermelon grown by grandpa is very sweet and really delicious.

ab: tjengelay aken uta ta uciya ni vuvu nu tekelen bulay angata.
ch: 我也喜歡爺爺的茶，也很好喝。
en: I also like grandpa’s tea, it’s tasted good too.

ab: nu vecekadan tu qadaw meseljang aken kati vuvu a vavayan a kemesa tua nia cengel.
ch: 中午的時候，我和奶奶一起煮午餐。
en: At noon, I cooked lunch with my grandma.

ab: kemesa ti vuvu tu liyaw angata cengelj izua ciqaw kuka siyak izua luli uta.
ch: 奶奶煮了很豐盛的午餐，有魚、有雞、有南瓜、還有蝸牛。
en: Grandma cooked a big lunch, including fish, chicken, pumpkin, and snails.

ab: kana kincengel anga men vaik amen a mapuljacengan a kiljaceng.
ch: 吃完午餐後，我們去菜園摘菜。
en: After lunch, we went to the vegetable garden to pick vegetables.

ab: liyaw a kini vurasi vasa rukiya ni vuvu tu sipakacu tjanuaken a tjumaq.
ch: 奶奶拿了很多地瓜、芋頭和蕗蕎給我，要我帶回家。
en: Grandma brought me a lot of sweet potatoes, taro and buckwheat, and asked me to take them home.

ab: kipusau aken tjayamadju kivalanga ken nu tjaivililj uta ku ayain a ikai.
ch: 我跟他們道別，說下次再去看他們。
en: I said goodbye to them and said I’d see them next time.
